title: Moving an International E-Commerce Warehouse in Addis: Inventory Flow and Multi-Truck Coordination
excerpt: E-commerce warehouse move Addis: international inventory flow, multi-truck coordination, SKU logistics, commercial relocation, fulfillment center transition Ethiopia.
content:
Moving an international e-commerce warehouse in Addis: inventory flow and multi-truck coordination

International e-commerce warehouses are living databases—thousands of SKUs, pick-path logic, and fulfillment SLAs that cannot pause because lease ended. Relocating warehouse operations in Addis demands inventory flow discipline, multi-truck choreography, and cutover planning that keeps orders shipping while pallets cross town.

This guide covers warehouse moves with inventory flow and multi-truck coordination.

Pre-move inventory architecture

  • Export WMS snapshot—SKU, bin, quantity, lot if applicable.
  • ABC classify: A-tier ships through move window from buffer stock.
  • Dead stock liquidate before truck—volume cost honesty.
  • Barcode or QR relabel plan if bin schema changes destination.

Multi-truck coordination model

Wave planning

Truck 1 bulk palletized slow movers; Truck 2 pick-face essentials; Truck 3 IT and packing stations. Stagger departures thirty minutes—avoid origin dock chaos.

Radio and lead roles

One warehouse lead origin, one destination, one dispatch—three-point command. Plate numbers broadcast to security both sites.

Dock and compound access

Book loading bays both ends; forklift hours aligned; pallet jack count sufficient. Addis industrial zones—freight truck parking pre-cleared with management.

Inventory custody during transit

Seal numbers logged per truck; manifest signed load and unload; spot-check SKU count high-value lines. CCTV handoff at destination before put-away.

IT and order cutover

Freeze pick window announced; redirect orders to alternate micro-fulfillment if exists; WMS location update before reopen pick. DNS and API endpoints for international platforms—parallel run until smoke test.

Staff shifts

Overtime crew destination put-away same day—empty truck useless if pallets sit dock overnight theft risk.

Active Movers commercial warehouse tier

Multi-truck quote with wave schedule annex; forklift crew optional line item.

Conclusion

E-commerce warehouse moves succeed with WMS-driven waves, sealed multi-truck manifests, dock booking both ends, and IT cutover parallelism—inventory flow continues when coordination beats single-truck hope.
author: Active Movers Team
